AuditChainBlastRadius.description: Blast-radius link of the custody chain: the models that transitively consume the subject, recomputed from the current compiled graph.

model.description: The model the blast radius was computed for.

transitive.description: All transitive downstream consumers (the full closure, sorted).

AuditChainDecisions.description: Decision link of the custody chain: the policy-decision ledger scoped to the subject (by model for a model selector, by plan_id for a plan selector).

AuditChainPlan.description: Plan link of the custody chain: what the governing plan changed, read from the plan file's embedded change-classification.

changes.description: The per-model change classification the plan carried â€” the persisted stand-in for the typed diff. The full field-level `diff_project_ir` output is not persisted for run plans, only this classification is.

diff_available.description: Whether the baseâ†”head change classification was available when the plan was authored. `false` means every planned model was treated as a breaking change (fail-closed), and `changes` reflects that.

plan_id.description: The plan that governed the subject (the newest one, for a model selector). `null` when no plan file could be located.

AuditChainRuns.description: Run link of the custody chain: runs that materialized the subject.

AuditChainVerify.description:
Verify-after link of the custody chain.

Post-apply verification outcomes are persisted to the decision ledger as custody rows with a non-empty `verify_after` check list â€” the two-step `rocky apply` gate writes one per verified apply, and the drift auto-apply path writes them under its `autoapply-verify:<run_id>` plan id. This link renders those rows for the subject's plan. When none exist the link says so plainly ("not recorded") â€” never a smoothed-over "verification passed".

total.description: Count of verification custody rows found for the subject.

AuditDecisionEntry.description: One recorded policy decision in the [`AuditOutput`] ledger.

model.description: The model the decision was about.

reason.description: Human-readable explanation of how the effect was reached.

rule_id.description: Index of the winning `[[policy.rules]]` entry, or `null` for the default posture.

timestamp.description: RFC 3339 timestamp when the decision was recorded.

capability.description: The change class the plan recorded for this model (`schema_change.additive` / `schema_change.breaking` / `value_change` / a bare verb).

triggering_identity.description: Best-effort caller identity recorded on the run.

AuditSubjectKind.description:
What `rocky audit --for <selector>` resolved its selector to.

The selector is resolved in priority order: a 64-char hex string with a plan file on disk is a [`AuditSubjectKind::Plan`]; a string matching a `run_id` in the run ledger is a [`AuditSubjectKind::Run`]; a string the decision ledger keys rows by is likewise a [`AuditSubjectKind::Plan`] (the plan file may be gone, or the id may be a decision-only custody key that never had one); anything else is treated as a [`AuditSubjectKind::Model`] name.

oneOf.description: A model / table name â€” the primary custody-chain entry point.
A `run_id` from the run ledger.

A `plan_id` â€” a 64-char blake3 hex with a plan file on disk, or any id the decision ledger keys rows by (including decision-only custody ids like `freeze:â€¦` / `draft:â€¦` / `autoapply:â€¦`, which never had a plan file).

AuditVerifyEntry.description:
One post-apply verification outcome inside [`AuditChainVerify`] â€” a decision-ledger custody row with a non-empty `verify_after` check list.

The ledger records the required check names, the aggregate verdict (`allow` = every named check passed, `deny` = a check failed or was absent and the apply halted), and a human-readable reason; per-check pass/fail detail beyond that lives only in the reason string, which is rendered verbatim rather than re-parsed.

checks.description: The named post-apply checks the verification required.

passed.description: Whether the verification passed (`allow` custody row) or failed (`deny`).

plan_id.description: The plan id the custody row was filed under (the applied plan, or the auto-apply path's `autoapply-verify:<run_id>`).

reason.description: The recorded outcome, verbatim from the custody row.

timestamp.description: RFC 3339 timestamp when the verification outcome was recorded.

PolicyCapability.description:
The class of action a policy rule governs.

`read` is always allowed (short-circuit). The mutating verbs (`propose` â€¦ `quarantine`) name coarse operations; `schema_change.additive`, `schema_change.breaking`, and `value_change` are *refinements* of the apply/promote verbs â€” a rule naming a bare verb (`apply`/`promote`) matches those refinements too, but a rule naming a refinement matches only that exact refinement.

oneOf.description: Read model output / metadata. Always allowed.
Draft a plan for later review.
Apply a plan against the warehouse.
Promote a branch / environment.
Backfill historical partitions.
Garbage-collect / reclaim storage.
Retry a failed run.
Quarantine a partition / model.
An additive schema change (refinement of apply/promote).
A breaking schema change (refinement of apply/promote).
A value-only data change (refinement of apply/promote).

PolicyEffect.description:
The verdict a policy rule (or the default posture) yields.

Ordered by restrictiveness for incomparable-rule tie-breaking: `Deny` is a hard override (handled separately), and among non-deny verdicts `RequireReview` is more restrictive than `Allow`.

oneOf.description: Permit the action outright.
Permit only after human review. The safe default posture.
Refuse the action. A hard override â€” no `allow` overturns it.

PolicyPrincipal.description:
Who is attempting an action.

`agent` is a non-human caller (an AI harness authoring, applying, or remediating). `human` is a person. In v0 the principal is supplied explicitly (`rocky policy check --principal â€¦`); auto-detection is a later phase.

oneOf.description: A person.
A non-human caller (AI agent / automation).

SectionAvailability.description:
Whether a brief section's underlying query succeeded and had data.

The digest is composed section-by-section from independent typed queries over the state store and the decision ledger. Each section fails closed: a query that returns nothing renders as [`SectionAvailability::NoData`], and a source that is not wired into the state store at all renders as [`SectionAvailability::Unavailable`] with a note â€” never as a smoothed-over "all clear". A brief that claims more than the ledger holds poisons the whole surface, so the marker is machine-readable, not prose.

oneOf.description: The query ran and the section carries data for the window.
The query ran cleanly but nothing fell inside the window.
The underlying signal is not recorded in the state store, so the section cannot be composed. Accompanied by a `note` explaining why.

description:
JSON output for `rocky audit --for <table|run|plan>` â€” the custody chain.

A one-query drill-down assembled link by link from the data Rocky already records: who proposed the change and what the policy plane decided ([`Self::decisions`]), what the plan changed ([`Self::plan`]), which runs materialized it ([`Self::runs`]), what a post-apply verification found ([`Self::verify_after`]), and what sits downstream in its blast radius ([`Self::blast_radius`]).

Every link fails closed the same way the estate brief does: a link whose signal is genuinely not recorded renders [`SectionAvailability::Unavailable`] with a note, never a fabricated or assumed value. Post-apply verification outcomes are persisted as decision-ledger custody rows (non-empty `verify_after`), so [`Self::verify_after`] renders them when they exist and says "not recorded" when none exist for the subject. The run ledger is not keyed to policy decisions, so a `run` selector cannot join back to a decision. The blast radius is recomputed from the current compiled graph (a live query, not a stored snapshot).

blast_radius.description: What sits downstream of the subject â€” the CLL blast radius.

decisions.description: Who proposed the change and what the policy plane decided â€” the persisted decision ledger, scoped to this subject.

plan.description: What the governing plan changed (its embedded per-model change classification).

resolved.description: Whether the selector matched anything at all (a decision, a run, a plan file, or a model in the graph). `false` when nothing referenced it â€” every link is then empty and says why.

subject.description: The selector as supplied on the command line.

verify_after.description: What a post-apply verification found â€” the verification custody rows recorded against the subject's plan.

title: AuditForOutput
